content.problemStatement:
Ash is trying to get visa to Chefland. For the visa to be approved, he needs to satisfy the following three criteria:


Solve at least x1x_1x1​ problems on Codechef.
Have at least y1y_1y1​ current rating on Codechef.
Make his last submission at most z1z_1z1​ months ago.
You are given the number of problems solved by Chef (x2x_2x2​), his current rating (y2y_2y2​) and the information that he made his last submission z2z_2z2​ months ago. Determine whether he will get the visa.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains six space‑separated integers: x1 x2 y1 y2 z1 z2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef satisfies all three criteria, otherwise "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Case 1: rating 1900 < required 2100 → NO. Case 2: problems solved 20 < required 50 → NO. Case 3: last submission 6 months ago > allowed 1 month → NO. Case 4: all three conditions satisfied → YES.
Chef meets problem count, rating and his last submission (2 ≤ 4). Hence visa is approved.
Rating 1999 is below required 2000, so visa is denied.

solution.approach: For each test case read the six integers. Chef gets the visa if and only if x2 >= x1 (solved enough problems), y2 >= y1 (rating high enough) and z2 <= z1 (last submission recent enough). Evaluate the three boolean conditions and print "YES" when all are true, otherwise "NO".
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
